hard.next_step: 6/30 - Excellent Discovery call with Cesar and his two Buyers. Demo scheduled for July 21st. Cesar will invite Accounting and Supplier managers. They are moving quickly. no specific timeline uncovered. Cesar will have more clarity on timeline by time of Demo Zycus: Discovery is booked for next Tuesday between 12 PM and 2 PM Pacific; Steve will send the recap, share the deck, include the solutions consultant, and prepare around eProcurement, sourcing, iContract, SRPM, and Merlin Intake. Cesar: Cesar will review the deck, invite one or two buyers, and share details on priorities, stakeholders, active evaluation, budget, and timeline.
hard.ais_why:
• At Marvin Engineering the 'Intake and Merlin Agentic Platform' was discussed with a 'positive reaction to automation and AI-enabled S2P capabilities' and 'interest in conversational AI interface of Merlin Intake.'
• The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is set to Yes, the deal is at Formal Evaluation, and evaluator Cesar 'confirmed strong need for Zycus modules.'
• ANA, Merlin and Merlin Intake are scoped and questions centered on modular purchasing, indicating a buyer actively planning an AI-enabled S2P adoption.

north_star_verdict.headline: Fresh, fast-moving indirect-procurement deal with a strong 30 June discovery and a demo booked for the week of 21 July, held back only by an unmapped economic buyer, an open timeline, and unvalidated SAP integration.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 25 June, 11 days old today (6 July). Close date 17 September gives 73 days to close. Entered Formal Evaluation 30 June; close date was pushed 23 July -> 17 September on 30 June (by the owner's manager), adding runway. Platform demo is booked for the week of 21 July (15 days out), leaving ~58 days after the demo for the formal quote, ROI business case, leadership approval, SAP-integration validation and contracting. No submission dates logged yet. Deal pulse is LIVE: intro call 24 June, deep discovery 30 June, demo scheduled 21 July, last two-way exchange 1 July. Pace is appropriate for an early Formal-Evaluation deal moving at buyer speed.
evidence: Excellent discovery call 30 June: Cesar Cherlin brought his two direct buyers Rafael Sanchez and Gloria McCoy and mapped the full indirect procure-to-pay landscape (Avoma discovery call, 30 Jun 2026).
Platform demo booked for the week of 21 July with accounting/AP and the quality/supplier-onboarding team to be invited (Next_Step__c 30 Jun 2026; Avoma discovery call, 30 Jun 2026).

Buyer is pulling the process forward: Cesar said 'we're moving very quick' as the business scales toward a $1B backlog and manual indirect procurement cannot keep up (Avoma discovery call, 30 Jun 2026).

No economic buyer engaged and no decision timeline yet: Cesar said budget green-light needs a formal quote taken to his director and executive leadership, and that the timeline 'might be some time' (Avoma discovery call, 30 Jun 2026).

metrics.narrative: Cesar Cherlin articulated rich qualitative pain on the 30 June discovery call but put no numbers to it. He described manual PO creation and receiving, non-PO invoices that leave accounts payable guessing ('accounts payable doesn't know what it is for'), maverick spend from emergency services, and a single-person supplier-onboarding bottleneck that 'comes to a complete halt' when that person is out. The only quantified context is business growth, not procurement cost: 'we were having maybe 3 to $400,000,000 backlog that now we're a couple mil away from a billion dollars,' which is why manual indirect procurement 'can't keep up.' No late-fee cost, buyer-hours, maverick-spend percentage or projected savings has been captured. Steve Ovadje offered to build a 5-year ROI business case after the demo ('our leadership loves metrics like that'), so quantifying the value is the immediate next move.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged and none is named in Salesforce; Cesar Cherlin is the sole contact role, at Evaluator. The call evidence shows budget authority sits above Cesar: he said 'I gotta get with my director... once I get a formal quote from you guys... then we're gonna have those internal discussions with my leadership, but maybe executive leadership because this is gonna be a complete overhaul,' casting himself as 'kind of the middleman.' So budget sign-off rests with Cesar's director (referenced only as 'Robert' on the call, unverified in Salesforce) and executive leadership, activated once a formal quote and business case are in hand. Mapping that economic buyer and securing a path to them, ideally via Cesar at or right after the demo, is the top early-stage move.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria emerged from the discovery conversation, not a formal scorecard. The buyer's stated priorities are: interoperability with SAP ('how is it gonna interact with SAP'), one end-to-end platform rather than point fixes (Ritika framed procurement as 'a domino effect... we want to solve that entire process, not just bits and pieces'), requisition intake directly on Microsoft Teams which they already use, catalogs for repeat MRO buys, sourcing with external supplier discovery so buyers do not rely on 'tribal knowledge,' a contract repository with proactive renewal alerts and negotiation guidance, and indirect-vs-direct supplier segmentation. Cesar validated the fit: 'everything you mentioned, that's what we're looking at.' No formal RFP, weighted scorecard or test-case framework has been shared yet, so criteria are confirmed in substance but not yet formalized.
sources: Avoma discovery call, 30 Jun 2026 (Cesar Cherlin, Ritika)

decision_process.narrative: The process reads directly off the call sequence: an intro call 24 June (Cesar with Zycus), a deep discovery 30 June where Cesar widened the room to his two buyers Rafael Sanchez and Gloria McCoy, and a platform demo booked for the week of 21 July to which Cesar committed to invite accounting/AP and the quality team that owns supplier onboarding. After the demo, Zycus provides a formal quote plus a 5-year ROI business case, which Cesar then takes into 'internal discussions with my leadership... maybe executive leadership' for the buy-in on a 'complete overhaul.' Cesar is the single conduit to power ('I'll be kind of the middleman'). No formal RFP round, vendor shortlist or approval chain has been disclosed, and Cesar said the decision timeline 'might be some time' while insisting 'we're moving very quick,' so the process is real but not yet locked.
sources: SF field history 30 Jun 2026 (Qualified -> Formal Evaluation)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics have not been discussed. As net-new full-suite business (eProcurement, sourcing, iSupplier, iContract, iRisk, Merlin/Merlin Intake, ANA) this will require an MSA, an Order Form and a SOW, plus SAP integration scoping and, given Aerospace & Defence and AS9100 supplier requirements, likely an InfoSec and IT review. No legal, procurement-channel, MSA/SOW or signature-authority detail exists yet. The only paper-trail signal on the call is that Marvin already uses DocuSign for supplier NDAs and stores them in SAP. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is unpopulated, so there is no expiring-contract forcing function. Visibility will come once the demo lands and the formal quote opens the commercial track; pulling IT into the demo is the way to surface the integration and security paper early.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed, specific and owned by Cesar Cherlin (Estimating & Procurement Manager). On 30 June he detailed: PRs manually tagged in SAP with manual emails to receiving; a supplier-onboarding process gated on one person where 'if that person's on vacation... it just comes to a complete halt' and buyers scramble onto credit cards; non-PO emergency invoices where 'accounts payable doesn't know what it is for'; no indirect supplier risk scoring ('we don't have any quality rating score' for indirect); no centralized contract repository so renewals are reactive ('two months come by... and all of a sudden I just get an email from IT... go negotiate this, and I got no time'); and heavy reliance on 'tribal knowledge' held by his two buyers. His two buyers confirmed the sourcing bottleneck: Gloria McCoy said the hardest part is 'trying to source an item out somewhere different, trying to get quotes from different suppliers,' and Rafael Sanchez flagged getting unapproved vendors approved. The pain spans procurement, accounts payable and the quality team.
sources: Avoma discovery call, 30 Jun 2026 (Cesar Cherlin, Gloria McCoy, Rafael Sanchez)

champion.narrative: Cesar Cherlin is a developing champion. What has developed: he initiated the evaluation (his director routed an inbound Zycus contact to him), ran an articulate discovery, widened the room to his two direct buyers, committed to bring accounting/AP and the quality/supplier team to the demo, and is visibly bought in ('I'm actually excited for all of this'). He is driving the cadence and framed himself as the internal owner of the change. What is still missing: he has not introduced Zycus to his director or executive leadership, has not shared a decision timeline or budget, and there is no competitor for him to advocate against yet, so his true influence over a full-suite spend is unproven. He is active and cooperative as of 1 July; strength will clarify by whether he delivers the promised cross-functional attendance and opens the path to leadership.

competition.narrative: This is effectively a sole-source situation whose real competitor is the status quo. Across the intro call, the 30 June discovery, the activities and the fields, the buyer named no third-party procure-to-pay rival; Cesar came inbound after a Zycus contact was routed through his director. The active alternative is 'do nothing / keep running indirect procurement manually,' which Marvin has done for years and which is the most likely failure mode given no compelling event and an open timeline. SAP is the incumbent ERP the solution must integrate with ('how is it gonna interact with SAP'), an integration dependency rather than a competing procurement suite. The wedge is a single automated platform landing the intake 'domino' (requisition-on-Teams through PO, sourcing, onboarding and contracts) plus Merlin Intake, positioned as the way out of tribal knowledge and single-person bottlenecks. Note: the prior record's SAP Ariba and Coupa entries were inferred with no buyer evidence and are retired here.

deal_movement.summary: Two-week-old deal moving fast at buyer pace: intro 24 June, stage to Qualified 25 June (owner set to Steve Ovadje), deep discovery 30 June with the stage advanced to Formal Evaluation and the close date pushed to 17 September, and a platform demo booked for the week of 21 July. Last two-way exchange was Cesar's 1 July email confirming the call. Pulse is LIVE.

items.change: Introductory call held between Zycus and Marvin Engineering
Stage advanced Initial Interest -> Qualified; opportunity re-owned to Steve Ovadje
Excellent discovery call with Cesar Cherlin plus buyers Rafael Sanchez and Gloria McCoy; stage advanced Qualified -> Formal Evaluation
Close date pushed 23 July -> 17 September, adding runway for demo, quote and leadership approval
Platform demo scheduled with accounting/AP and quality/supplier managers to be invited

competitive_position.summary: No third-party procure-to-pay rival has been named on any call, field or activity; the single strongest current threat is inertia. Marvin has run indirect procurement manually inside SAP for years, so a no-decision (status quo) is the realistic failure mode, not a loss to a competitor. SAP is the incumbent ERP and an integration dependency, not a competing suite. Win by landing the single-platform intake domino and Merlin Intake before the initiative loses urgency.

competitors.name: Status quo / manual processes (do-nothing)
competitors.quote: We're just so behind on the indirect that there's no way of us pretty much doing exactly what you're talking about... whatever helps me ease all of that, we'll take it.
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor the intake 'domino' (requisition-on-Teams -> PO -> sourcing -> onboarding -> contracts) and Merlin Intake as the escape from tribal knowledge and single-person bottlenecks; tie urgency to the $1B backlog scale-up.

competitors.name: SAP (ERP incumbent - integration dependency, not a procurement rival)
competitors.quote: how is it gonna interact with SAP
competitors.how_we_win: Prove clean SAP interoperability in the demo and pull IT in early so integration is a strength, not a doubt.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.retire_reason: Never named by the buyer on any call, field or activity; the prior entry was inferred from the SAP ERP stack, not stated. No evidence of an SAP Ariba evaluation.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.retire_reason: Never named by the buyer on any call, field or activity; the prior entry was an unsupported market-dynamics inference.

best_practice_check.summary: Early Formal-Evaluation deal, single-threaded into procurement, with a strong champion but no power access, no quantified value and an unvalidated SAP integration. Highest-leverage levers: widen the room, quantify the value, and map the budget owner before the quote lands.
flags: Multi-thread beyond procurement: get IT/SAP, an AP stakeholder and the quality/supplier-onboarding owner into the 21 July demo to de-risk integration and widen sponsorship.
Quantify the value now: convert Cesar's rich pain (single-person onboarding halts, non-PO invoices, reactive renewals) into a hard-number ROI his leadership can act on.
Map the economic buyer early: budget sits with Cesar's director and executive leadership, so secure Cesar's path to them before the formal quote is delivered.

items.name: Cesar Cherlin
items.title: Estimating & Procurement Manager
items.risk: Single conduit to power; has not yet introduced Zycus to his director or executive leadership, and no budget or timeline confirmed
items.sentiment: Engaged and enthusiastic; drove discovery, widened the room, committed to bring cross-functional stakeholders ('I'm actually excited for all of this')

champion_strength.summary: Cesar Cherlin is a developing champion: he initiated the evaluation, ran an articulate discovery, brought his two buyers, and is visibly bought in and driving cadence. Still missing: an introduction to his director/executive leadership, a shared timeline and budget, and any tested influence over a full-suite spend. Active as of 1 July.
champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole: Cesar Cherlin, Evaluator; Avoma discovery call, 30 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS 7 / Moderate, corroborated by a positive reaction to Merlin Intake and the conversational AI interface. The rep intentionally sequenced core automation ahead of advanced AI ('we can look at some advanced AI features at later stages, which is not necessary'), a sound choice for a foundational buyer, but the Merlin Intake on-Teams angle should still lead the demo since it maps to their intake pain.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 7 / Moderate. Calls show genuine but early AI curiosity: positive reaction to Merlin Intake and the conversational AI interface, plus intake directly on Microsoft Teams. Appetite is real but foundational-first, with advanced AI intentionally deferred by the rep.

items.detail: No economic buyer engaged; budget sign-off sits with Cesar's director and executive leadership, and Cesar is the middleman who cannot approve a full-suite overhaul.
SAP ERP integration is asserted as a requirement but unvalidated, and no IT stakeholder has engaged.
Single-threaded into procurement: Cesar, Rafael and Gloria are all in the same team; AP, quality and IT have not yet joined.
Full-suite overhaul across procurement, AP and quality; buyer wants a phased indirect-first test run, and today's process leans on single-person and tribal-knowledge dependencies.
No decision or go-live timeline; Cesar said it 'might be some time' pending a formal quote reaching leadership.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and warming: intro 24 June, deep discovery 30 June, demo booked for the week of 21 July, buyer pulling the process forward. Not cooling.
items: Buyer actively scheduled the demo and asked to bring more stakeholders (Avoma 30 Jun 2026).
Cesar confirmed the follow-up call by email on 1 July (SF activity).
Cesar framed the initiative as urgent given the scale-up toward a $1B backlog (Avoma 30 Jun 2026).

scope_change.to: Indirect procurement first, direct procurement to follow
scope_change.detail: Cesar scoped indirect procurement as a change-managed test run with explicit intent to expand into direct once live ('we might start off with indirect... then eventually go over into the direct procurement'). This is a phased rollout with expansion upside, not a scope cut (Avoma discovery call, 30 Jun 2026).

customer_preference.evidence: Cesar asked Zycus-specific next steps (a tailored demo, a formal quote, a 5-year ROI doc) and said 'everything you mentioned, that's what we're looking at,' but named no competitor to compare against and set no decision timeline (Avoma 30 Jun 2026).

business_case.evidence: Rich qualitative pain (manual PO/receiving, non-PO invoices, single-person supplier-onboarding halts, no contract repository, no indirect supplier risk scoring) plus business-growth urgency toward a $1B backlog, but no procurement cost, hours or savings quantified yet (Avoma 30 Jun 2026).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Fresh, fast-moving indirect-procurement deal: Cesar Cherlin ran an excellent 30 June discovery with his two buyers and booked a 21 July platform demo, but with no economic buyer engaged, no decision timeline and no quantified ROI it sits early in Formal Evaluation, where the real competitor is inertia.

win_position.text: We are well-placed early: a strong 30 June discovery mapped the full indirect procure-to-pay pain, Cesar Cherlin brought his two direct buyers, and a tailored platform demo is booked for the week of 21 July (Avoma, 30 Jun).
No economic buyer is engaged: budget sign-off sits with Cesar's director and executive leadership, and Cesar is only the middleman who cannot approve a full-suite overhaul himself (Avoma, 30 Jun).
There is no decision timeline and no quantified ROI yet, so the deal can still stall to a no-decision as the initiative competes with the day-to-day (Avoma, 30 Jun).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is strong for an 11-day-old deal: intro 24 June, deep discovery 30 June, and a demo already scheduled for the week of 21 July with accounting/AP and the quality team to be invited (Avoma, 30 Jun).
Cesar is pulling the process forward, saying 'we're moving very quick' as the business scales toward a $1B backlog and manual indirect procurement cannot keep up (Avoma, 30 Jun).
The customer named no decision date; the demo date is set but the buy-decision timeline stays open ('it might be some time'), so momentum rests on our cadence (Avoma, 30 Jun).

customer_commitment.text: Cesar committed to widen the buying group, inviting accounts payable and the quality team that owns supplier onboarding to the demo (Avoma, 30 Jun).
Commitment is still procurement-level only: no executive or finance budget owner has engaged, and engagement rests on one champion plus his two buyers (Avoma, 30 Jun).

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded into procurement: Cesar, Rafael Sanchez and Gloria McCoy are all in the same team; IT/SAP, finance and quality have not yet joined (Avoma, 30 Jun).
SAP ERP integration is unvalidated and no IT stakeholder has engaged, leaving a technical fit question open into the demo (Avoma, 30 Jun).
The real competitor is inertia: Marvin has run indirect procurement manually for years, so a no-decision remains the most likely failure mode (Avoma, 30 Jun).

champion_access.evidence: Cesar is engaged and driving but has not opened access to his director or executive leadership (Avoma 30 Jun).

single_thread.evidence: All three engaged contacts sit in procurement; AP, quality and IT not yet involved (Avoma 30 Jun).

value_quantified.evidence: Pain is rich but no ROI or savings quantified yet (Avoma 30 Jun).

expansion_context.note: Net-new business; no prior Closed-Won deal on Marvin Engineering. No inherited executive access, so an unmapped economic buyer is a genuine early-stage gap here.

Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 33 is carried by momentum 58 vs stage-expected 52; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 58 (moving forward): customer requested next meeting (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 26 is anchored on the stage win (33), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 58, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
